refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Refining it into sub-FRs focused on monitoring energy use, controlling operations to reduce energy, and optimizing overall system performance aligns with the energy efficiency KPI and supports verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and comparing to E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy; confirm control actions reduce energy use; demonstrate optimization algorithms improve efficiency.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy use per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system parameters and operational strategies to achieve energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and load sensors integrated with telemetry for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive control algorithms in vehicle control systems to reduce energy consumption.
DP3: Use data analytics and feedback loops in the NGHE solution to optimize operational parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring and control modules
Feedback loops between optimization and control
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and managed via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking). The design parameters are logically cohesive and modular.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor data and reliable telemetry links.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting real-time responsiveness.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of data and model accuracy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing optimization separately to allow iterative improvements without impacting control stability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level implementation including data analytics, optimization algorithms, and system integration.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time energy and load data from Vehicle sensors.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends control commands to Vehicle for energy-efficient operation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes optimization and analytics, minimizing diffusion.
Contains control algorithms but shares telemetry data externally.
Limited containment for energy efficiency functions.
No direct role in energy efficiency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency functions are best contained within the NGHE Solution module to centralize data analytics and optimization, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential latency in telemetry data affecting real-time control.
Complexity of integrating control algorithms across Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Enhance interface definitions and data synchronization mechanisms between Vehicle and NGHE Solution to mitigate latency and integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well decomposed and mapped to logical DPs that address monitoring, control, and optimization aspects of energy efficiency.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for control and telemetry data sharing.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor centralizing analytics and optimization in NGHE Solution, with control logic partly in Vehicle.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data validation, control command logs, and energy efficiency KPI measurements.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface protocols to reduce latency and improve data integrity.
Consider modularizing control algorithms to balance real-time responsiveness and maintainability.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meets the requirements and passes gate reviews with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy use per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system parameters and operational strategies to achieve energy efficiency target.

revise.why: No major revisions needed at this level; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Reliable telemetry and sensor data availability.
Sufficient computational resources in NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
open_risks: Latency and integration complexity between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Control algorithm complexity impacting real-time performance.
evidence_needed: Energy efficiency KPI measurement over evaluation period.
